title: O Tempo e as Rotinas
subtitle: Weather, Seasons & Daily Routines
description: Learn weather vocabulary, seasons, and how to talk about daily routines using reflexive verbs.

vocabulary.word: Está a chover / Está a fazer sol
vocabulary.pronunciation: esh-TAH ah shoh-VAIR / esh-TAH ah fah-ZER sol
vocabulary.example: Hoje está a chover. Amanhã vai fazer sol.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: Today it's raining. Tomorrow it will be sunny.
vocabulary.tip: Weather uses ESTAR A + INFINITIVO: está a chover (it's raining), está a nevar (it's snowing)

vocabulary.example: No inverno está frio. No verão está muito calor.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: In winter it's cold. In summer it's very hot.
vocabulary.tip: Use 'está' for current weather conditions. 'Frio' and 'calor' are nouns here, not adjectives
'Nublado' is an adjective. For wind, use 'fazer vento' (to make wind)

vocabulary.word: a primavera / o verão / o outono / o inverno
vocabulary.pronunciation: ah pree-mah-VEH-rah / oh veh-RAH-nyoo / oh oh-TOH-noh / oh een-VEHR-nyoo
vocabulary.example: A primavera é a minha estação preferida.
vocabulary.tip: Only 'primavera' is feminine. The others are masculine: o verão, o outono, o inverno

vocabulary.translation: To get up / To wake up
vocabulary.tip: Reflexive verb: levanto-me, levantas-te, levanta-se, levantamo-nos, levantam-se
Irregular in 1st person: visto (not 'vesto'). visto-me, vestes-te, veste-se...
Regular reflexive: lavo-me, lavas-te, lava-se, lavamo-nos, lavam-se
Stem-changing verb: deito-me, deitas-te, deita-se, deitamo-nos, deitam-se

vocabulary.example: — Que horas são? — São duas e meia.
vocabulary.tip: Use SER for telling time. Plural for 1:00+ (são), singular for 1:00 (é uma)

vocabulary.word: São duas e meia / É uma e um quarto
vocabulary.pronunciation: sowng DOO-ash ee MAY-ah / eh OO-mah ee oom KWAHR-too
vocabulary.ipa: /ˈsɐ̃w̃ ˈduɐz i ˈmejɐ/ / /ˈɛ ˈumɐ i ũ ˈkwartu/
vocabulary.example: O comboio chega às duas e meia.
vocabulary.tip: e meia = and a half (30 min). e um quarto = and a quarter (15 min). menos um quarto = quarter to

vocabulary.word: ao fim de semana / durante a semana
vocabulary.translation: On the weekend / During the week
vocabulary.pronunciation: oh feeng deh seh-MAH-nah / doo-RAHN-teh ah seh-MAH-nah
vocabulary.example: Ao fim de semana descanso. Durante a semana trabalho.
vocabulary.exampleTranslation: On the weekend I rest. During the week I work.

grammar.title: Weather, Seasons, Daily Routines & Reflexive Verbs

sections.heading: Talking About the Weather
sections.body: In Portuguese, weather expressions use the verb ESTAR + A + INFINITIVE: 'Está a chover' (It's raining), 'Está a nevar' (It's snowing), 'Está a fazer sol' (It's sunny). For temperature, use 'está' with nouns: 'Está frio' (It's cold), 'Está calor' (It's hot). You can also use 'fazer': 'Faz frio' (It's cold), 'Faz calor' (It's hot). Both 'está frio' and 'faz frio' are correct — 'está' emphasizes the current moment, 'fazer' is more general.

sections.heading: The Four Seasons
sections.body: Portuguese seasons: a primavera (spring — feminine), o verão (summer), o outono (autumn), o inverno (winter). To say something happens in a particular season, use 'em' which contracts: na primavera (em + a), no verão (em + o), no outono, no inverno. Example: 'No verão faz muito calor' (In summer it's very hot).

sections.heading: Reflexive Verbs for Daily Routines
sections.body: Reflexive verbs are essential for describing daily routines. They indicate that the subject performs the action on themselves. The reflexive pronoun changes with the subject: me (myself), te (yourself), se (himself/herself), nos (ourselves), se (themselves). Common daily routine reflexive verbs: levantar-se (get up), lavar-se (wash), vestir-se (get dressed), deitar-se (go to bed), pentear-se (comb hair), sentar-se (sit down). In European Portuguese, the pronoun usually goes AFTER the verb connected by a hyphen: 'Levanto-me às 7h'.

items.translation: I get up at 7 and wash (myself).
items.pronunciation: ay-oo leh-VAHN-too-meh ahsh SEH-teh ee LAH-voo-meh.

items.portuguese: Ela veste-se e penteia-se antes do pequeno-almoço.
items.translation: She gets dressed and combs her hair before breakfast.
items.pronunciation: EH-lah VESH-teh-seh ee pen-TEH-ah-seh AHN-tesh doo peh-KAY-noh ahl-MOH-soo.
nohsh day-TAH-moosh-meh ahsh OH-nyeh dah NOY-teh.

sections.heading: Telling Time in Portuguese
sections.body: To tell time in Portuguese, use the verb SER: 'É uma' (It's one o'clock — singular), 'São duas/três...' (It's two/three... — plural for 2+). For half hours: 'e meia' (and a half). For quarters: 'e um quarto' (and a quarter past), 'menos um quarto' (quarter to). For specific times with 'at', use 'à' (singular) or 'às' (plural): 'à uma hora' (at 1:00), 'às duas horas' (at 2:00). In Portugal, the 24-hour clock is commonly used in writing and formal contexts.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes & Tips
items: Weather: 'Está a chover' (not 'É chover'). The structure is ESTAR + A + INFINITIVE.
For temperature, both 'está frio' and 'faz frio' are correct. 'Está' = right now, 'faz' = general.
Reflexive pronouns go AFTER the verb in European Portuguese: 'Levanto-me' (not 'Me levanto').
The verb 'vestir' is irregular in the 1st person: 'visto' (not 'vesto'). Same pattern as 'pedir', 'seguir'.
Telling time: use singular 'É' only for 1:00. Everything else uses 'São': São duas, São três...
Seasons: only 'primavera' is feminine. Use 'no' for all others: no verão, no outono, no inverno.
For 'quarter to' the hour, use 'menos um quarto': 2:45 = São três menos um quarto.

exercises.title: Choose the Correct Weather Expression
exercises.instructions: Select the correct way to describe the weather.

items.question: It's raining.
items.explanation: Weather uses ESTAR + A + INFINITIVE: 'Está a chover' = It's raining.

items.question: It's cold today.
items.explanation: For temperature, use 'está' + noun: 'Está frio' = It's cold. You can also say 'Faz frio'.

items.question: It's sunny.
items.explanation: 'Está a fazer sol' = It's sunny (literally: it's making sun). This is the standard expression.

exercises.title: Complete the Reflexive Verb
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank with the correct reflexive form.

items.hint: 2nd person singular of vestir-se (irregular: vestes, not 'vistas')

items.question: How do you say 2:30?
items.explanation: 'São duas e meia' = It's 2:30. 'E meia' = and a half. Use 'São' (plural) for 2:00+.

items.question: How do you say 1:00?
items.explanation: 'É uma' = It's 1:00. Use singular 'É' only for 1:00.

items.question: How do you say 3:45?
items.explanation: 'São quatro menos um quarto' = It's 3:45 (four o'clock minus a quarter).

exercises.title: Describe Your Daily Routine
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct reflexive form to complete each sentence.

items.sentence: Nós ___ cedo ao fim de semana. (deitar-se)
